title: Constipation (starter evidence summary)
text:
Constipation is commonly defined as infrequent bowel movements, hard stools, or straining.
Telephone triage focuses on identifying red flags for obstruction, GI bleeding, or severe illness.

Red flags needing urgent evaluation:
- Severe or worsening abdominal pain
- Persistent vomiting or inability to keep fluids down
- Inability to pass gas with abdominal distension
- Black/tarry stools or large-volume rectal bleeding
- Fever with significant abdominal tenderness

Supportive home-care (when no red flags):
- Encourage hydration if safe, fiber intake, and gentle activity.
- Consider OTC options (e.g., stool softener or osmotic laxative) only if safe for the patient; avoid dosing instructions by phone.
- Seek in-person care if symptoms persist, worsen, or new red flags appear.
